content:
The user wants me to extract the table from the provided image. **1. Identify the table structure:** The image contains a table titled "Table 4 Test condition, procedure and requirements". The table has four main columns: - TEST - TEST METHOD - PROCEDURE - REQUIREMENTS **2. Analyze the rows:** - **Row 1 (Header):** TEST, TEST METHOD, PROCEDURE, REQUIREMENTS - **Row 2:** Temperature Coefficient of Resistance (T.C.R.) - Test Method: MIL-STD-202F-method 304; JIS C 5202-4.8 - Procedure: At +25/-55 °C and +25/+125 °C - Formula: $T.C.R. = \frac{R_2 - R_1}{R_1(t_2 - t_1)} \times 10^6$ (ppm/°C) - Where: - $t_1 = +25$ °C or specified room temperature - $t_2 = -55$ °C or +125 °C test temperature - $R_1$ = resistance at reference temperature in ohms - $R_2$ = resistance at test temperature in ohms - Requirements: Refer to table 3 - **Row 3:** Thermal Shock - Test Method: MIL-STD-202F-method 107G; IEC 60115-1 4.19 - Procedure: At -65 (+0/-10) °C for 2 minutes and at +155 (+10/-0) °C for 2 minutes; 25 cycles - Requirements: ±(0.5%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol. ±(1.0%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol. - **Row 4:** Low Temperature Operation - Test Method: MIL-R-55342D-Para 4.7.4 - Procedure: At -65 (+0/-5) °C for 1 hour; RCWV applied for 45 (+5/-0) minutes - Requirements: ±(0.5%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol. ±(1.0%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol. No visible damage - **Row 5:** Short Time Overload - Test Method: MIL-R-55342D-Para 4.7.5; IEC 60115-1 4.13 - Procedure: $2.5 \times$ RCWV applied for 5 seconds at room temperature - Requirements: ±(1.0%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol. ±(2.0%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol. No visible damage - **Row 6:** Insulation Resistance - Test Method: MIL-STD-202F-method 302; IEC 60115-1 4.6.1.1 - Procedure: RCOV for 1 minute - Type: RC0805 - Voltage (DC): 300 V - Requirements: ≥10 GΩ - **Row 7:** Dielectric Withstand Voltage - Test Method: MIL-STD-202F-method 301; IEC 60115-1 4.6.1.1 - Procedure: Maximum voltage ($V_{rms}$) applied for 1 minute - Type: RC0805 - Voltage (AC): 300 $V_{rms}$ - Requirements: No breakdown or flashover - **Row 8:** Resistance to Soldering Heat - Test Method: MIL-STD-202F-method 210C; IEC 60115-1 4.18 - Procedure: Unmounted chips; 260 ±5 °C for 10 ±1 seconds - Requirements: ±(0.5%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol. ±(1.0%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol. No visible damage - **Row 9:** Life - Test Method: MIL-STD-202F-method 108A; IEC 60115-1 4.25.1 - Procedure: At 70±2 °C for 1,000 hours; RCWV applied for 1.5 hours on and 0.5 hour off - Requirements: ±(1%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol. ±(3%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol. **3. Format the output:** I will create a markdown table representing this data. I'll need to handle the nested table structure for "Type" and "Voltage" within the "Procedure" column. **4. Refine the content:** - Ensure mathematical formulas are represented correctly. - Ensure units are correct. - Ensure all text is transcribed accurately. Let's construct the markdown. ```markdown | TEST | TEST METHOD | PROCEDURE | REQUIREMENTS | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | Temperature Coefficient of Resistance (T.C.R.) | MIL-STD-202F-method 304; JIS C 5202-4.8 | At +25/-55 °C and +25/+125 °C
Formula:
$T.C.R. = \frac{R_2 - R_1}{R_1(t_2 - t_1)} \times 10^6$ (ppm/°C)
Where
$t_1 = +25$ °C or specified room temperature
$t_2 = -55$ °C or +125 °C test temperature
$R_1$ = resistance at reference temperature in ohms
$R_2$ = resistance at test temperature in ohms | Refer to table 3 | | Thermal Shock | MIL-STD-202F-method 107G; IEC 60115-1 4.19 | At -65 (+0/-10) °C for 2 minutes and at +155 (+10/-0) °C for 2 minutes; 25 cycles | ±(0.5%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol. ±(1.0%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol. | | Low Temperature Operation | MIL-R-55342D-Para 4.7.4 | At -65 (+0/-5) °C for 1 hour; RCWV applied for 45 (+5/-0) minutes | ±(0.5%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol. ±(1.0%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol. No visible damage | | Short Time Overload | MIL-R-55342D-Para 4.7.5; IEC 60115-1 4.13 | $2.5 \times$ RCWV applied for 5 seconds at room temperature | ±(1.0%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol. ±(2.0%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol. No visible damage | | Insulation Resistance | MIL-STD-202F-method 302; IEC 60115-1 4.6.1.1 | RCOV for 1 minute
Type RC0805
Voltage (DC) 300 V | ≥10 GΩ | | Dielectric Withstand Voltage | MIL-STD-202F-method 301; IEC 60115-1 4.6.1.1 | Maximum voltage ($V_{rms}$) applied for 1 minute
Type RC0805
Voltage (AC) 300 $V_{rms}$ | No breakdown or flashover | | Resistance to Soldering Heat | MIL-STD-202F-method 210C; IEC 60115-1 4.18 | Unmounted chips; 260 ±5 °C for 10 ±1 seconds | ±(0.5%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol. ±(1.0%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol. No visible damage | | Life | MIL-STD-202F-method 108A; IEC 60115-1 4.25.1 | At 70±2 °C for 1,000 hours; RCWV applied for 1.5 hours on and 0.5 hour off | ±(1%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol. ±(3%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol. | ``` I will double check the transcription. - The formula for T.C.R. looks correct. - The conditions for Thermal Shock look correct. - The conditions for Low Temperature Operation look correct. - The conditions for Short Time Overload look correct. - The Insulation Resistance section has a nested table structure for Type and Voltage. - The Dielectric Withstand Voltage section has a nested table structure for Type and Voltage. - The Resistance to Soldering Heat section looks correct. - The Life section looks correct. The bottom left has some icons and text "Sep 03, 2004 V.2". The bottom right has "www.yageo.com". The top right has "Product specification" and a page number "7/10". The top left has "YAGEO". The header row has "Chip Resistor Surface Mount", "RC", "SERIES", "0805 (Pb Free)". I will include the main table content as requested. The user asked for the table content. I will provide the markdown table. I will also include the title "Table 4 Test condition, procedure and requirements". I will also include the title "TESTS AND REQUIREMENTS". Final check on the formula: $T.C.R. = \frac{R_2 - R_1}{R_1(t_2 - t_1)} \times 10^6$ (ppm/°C). Yes, that matches the image. Final check on the requirements for Thermal Shock: ±(0.5%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol. ±(1.0%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol. Yes. Final check on the requirements for Life: ±(1%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol. ±(3%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol. Yes. The table is quite long, so I need to make sure the markdown is clean. I will use `
` for line breaks within cells. I will use standard markdown table syntax. I will use LaTeX for the formula. I will use subscripts for $t_1$, $t_2$, $R_1$, $R_2$, $V_{rms}$. One detail: In the "Insulation Resistance" row, under "Procedure", there is a small table for "Type" and "Voltage (DC)". Type | RC0805 Voltage (DC) | 300 V Similarly for "Dielectric Withstand Voltage", under "Procedure", there is a small table for "Type" and "Voltage (AC)". Type | RC0805 Voltage (AC) | 300 $V_{rms}$ I will represent these as nested tables or just text with line breaks. Text with line breaks is probably safer for general markdown compatibility, but a nested table is more accurate to the visual layout. I'll use a nested table for clarity. Wait, looking closely at the image, for "Insulation Resistance", the "Procedure" column contains: "RCOV for 1 minute" Then a small table: Type | RC0805 Voltage (DC) | 300 V For "Dielectric Withstand Voltage", the "Procedure" column contains: "Maximun voltage ($V_{rms}$) applied for 1 minute" Then a small table: Type | RC0805 Voltage (AC) | 300 $V_{rms}$ I will format these as nested tables. One final check on the "Life" row requirements. ±(1%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol. ±(3%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol. Yes, that matches. Okay, ready to generate the response. I will start with the title "TESTS AND REQUIREMENTS". Then "Table 4 Test condition, procedure and requirements". Then the table. I will omit the header information (YAGEO, Product specification, etc.) as it's not part of the table data itself, but the prompt asks to "extract the table", so I should focus on the table. However, providing context is usually helpful. I'll stick to the table content primarily. Actually, the prompt is just "extract the table". I will provide the markdown for the table. One small correction: In the "Thermal Shock" row, the procedure says "At -65 (+0/-10) °C for 2 minutes and at +155 (+10/-0) °C for 2 minutes; 25 cycles". In the "Low Temperature Operation" row, the procedure says "At -65 (+0/-5) °C for 1 hour; RCWV applied for 45 (+5/-0) minutes". In the "Short Time Overload" row, the procedure says "$2.5 \times$ RCWV applied for 5 seconds at room temperature". Everything looks good. I will use `&` for the nested table cells. I will use `
` for line breaks. I will use `$$` for the formula. Let's do this.
